records.text:
What is Scrolless and how does it work?
Scrolless is an iOS app designed to protect your eyes from digital strain. Using Apple's Screen Time API,
Scrolless monitors your phone usage and prompts you to take science-backed eye rest breaks at optimal
intervals.
Here's how it works:
● Scrolless tracks your time on selected apps
● Our algorithm calculates when your eyes need rest based on your usage
● When it's time for a break, we lock the tracked app and send you a notification
● You take a short guided rest session
● The Eye Strain Meter shows your strain levels throughout the day
It's automatic eye care that fits seamlessly into your daily routine.

How much does Scrolless cost?
Scrolless offers a 7 days free trial period so you can experience the full benefits risk-free. After the trial,
regular annual subscription is: [price]
All subscriptions are managed through the App Store and can be cancelled anytime.

How do I set up and use the Scrolless app?
It's very simple:
1. Install the app on your iPhone
2. Choose which apps Scrolless should track. For the best eye protection, we recommend selecting
all or most of your apps. However, you may want to exclude essential apps such as those used
for work, video calls, or navigation.
3. That's it! Use your phone as usual, and our smart algorithm will take care of the rest.
From time to time, Scrolless will lock your screen when it's time to rest and invite you to take a break.
Simply tap the notification to start your rest session—or press Skip if you can't take a break right away.
Our Eye Strain Meter will monitor your strain levels throughout the day and show your results after each
rest session. You can also check your current strain level anytime by opening Scrolless.

Is Scrolless available for Android, Mac, or Windows?
Not yet—but we're working on it! For now, Scrolless is only available for iPhone.
Want to be the first to know when new versions launch? [Sign up for updates here], and we'll keep you
informed.
Features & Benefits

When will I start noticing relief from eye strain?
The timeframe varies depending on your overall eye health, but most people experience improvement in
their eye strain symptoms within 1-14 days.
Individuals with significant visual fatigue or imbalance may feel noticeable relief in as little as 1-3 days,
while those with mild or occasional eye strain might notice results more slowly. Allow your eyes time to
adapt to a new, more balanced and rested routine.

Is Scrolless safe to use?
Taking regular breaks from screens is the most natural way to rest them and, as a result, is the most
recommended simple way to reduce digital eye strain. Taking breaks from screens is completely safe for
your eyes.
Just use common sense—avoid closing your eyes in situations where it could be dangerous, such as
while driving or operating machinery.

Can I customize when and how often I get break reminders?
Currently, Scrolless uses a smart algorithm that calculates optimal break timing based on your individual
usage patterns and eye strain levels. This ensures you get breaks when you actually need them, rather
than at arbitrary fixed intervals.

Which apps should I track with Scrolless?
For comprehensive eye protection, we recommend tracking all apps or at least all apps where you spend
significant time scrolling, reading, or watching content.
You may want to exclude:
● Navigation apps (Maps, Waze)
● Video calling apps (Teams, Zoom) if you use them for work
● Work-critical apps that you cannot interrupt
Tip: You can always adjust your tracked apps list later in Settings (tap the Profile tab in the top-right
corner).

What if my eye strain persists despite using Scrolless?
Your eyes are complicated and delicate organs, so it's important to care for them properly. Scrolless is
just one part of maintaining healthy vision while using your mobile device.
Other factors that affect eye health:
● Overall screen time across multiple devices (computers, tablets, TV)
● Air quality and lighting conditions
● Nutrition and hydration
● Time spent outdoors
● Age and general health conditions
If you regularly experience eye strain, fatigue, irritation, or other symptoms, consider consulting your GP
or ophthalmologist. Remember, Scrolless is NOT a medical care provider, and persistent eye strain may
indicate underlying eye conditions. In such cases, addressing the root cause will be more effective than
relying on relaxation alone.
Privacy & Performance

Does Scrolless see or store my Screen Time data?
No. Your Screen Time data always stays on your device. On iOS, we use Apple's Screen Time API to
invite you to rest your eyes while you're using other apps—we never store any of this data; it remains
entirely on your phone.
As for other data, like most technology companies, we use limited information to improve our products
and services. As an EU-based company, we prioritize your privacy—even when collecting more data
could benefit product development—and follow strict EU GDPR rules. You can find our detailed Privacy
Policy here.
One more thing: We never, ever sell any of your personal data to third parties. Scrolless is built by and
for individuals. Our users are not the product—Scrolless is.

Will Scrolless affect my phone's performance or battery life?
No, Scrolless is designed to run efficiently in the background without slowing down your phone or
draining your battery.
It uses iOS Screen Time data rather than heavy processing, so the impact on performance and battery is
minimal. Most users won't notice any difference in their phone's battery life.
Troubleshooting
I don't receive notifications to take breaks
If your tracked app gets locked but you don't receive a notification to rest your eyes - even after
requesting it to be resent - it is most likely being filtered out by your phone's Focus mode. (Focus is an
Apple feature that limits notifications and distractions.)
To allow notifications from Scrolless:
1. Go to Settings on your iPhone
2. Tap Focus
3. For each Focus mode you use, add Scrolless to the list of allowed apps

What if break notifications disappear too quickly?
We use iPhone's default notification length, which might be too short for some users. If the notification
disappears before you tap it, you can always click Resend notification at the bottom of the Scrolless
screen lock.
To make notifications stay visible until you act on them:
1. Go to your iPhone's Settings
2. Tap Notifications
3. Choose Scrolless from the list of apps
4. Under Banner style, select Persistent
Now, every Scrolless break reminder will remain on your screen until you tap it.
I never get reminders for a specific app, even though it's selected as tracked
Occasionally, screen locks prompting you to rest may not work on one or a few apps you have selected.
Here's how to fix the issue:
Step 1: Check the list of Tracked Apps
Confirm that the apps you want to track are indeed selected in Tracked apps (Tap the Profile tab at the
top-right corner to access Settings). If this doesn't resolve the issue:
Step 2: Review your "Always Allowed" Apps
Your iPhone Screen Time Settings have an Always Allowed list of apps, which Apple generates
automatically (and may include apps you've selected). Apps in this category will always be allowed and
override any rules Scrolless applies.
To troubleshoot:
1. Go to your iPhone Settings
2. Tap Screen Time
3. Tap Always Allowed
4. If an app you want to track is listed under Allowed Apps, remove it

How do I avoid interruptions during video calls or while driving?
Scrolless calculates your time on all tracked apps and prompts you to rest whenever a break is due. If you
don't want to be interrupted while using essential apps, there are two ways to exclude them:
Easy Way
Unselect the apps you don't want to be disturbed on in your Tracked Apps list in Settings (tap the Profile
tab in the top-right corner).
You won't get Scrolless screen locks or receive break reminders while using these apps. However, keep in
mind that time spent on these apps will not be included in your overall eye strain calculation, nor
reflected in daily Eye Strain Meter values.
Advanced Way
Your iPhone's Screen Time Settings have an Always Allowed list of apps, which Apple generates
automatically and to which you can add apps manually. Apps in this category will never be locked by
Scrolless, but time spent on them will still count toward your overall eye strain calculation.
To use this option:
1. Select all apps and categories for Scrolless to track in Scrolless Settings
2. Go to your iPhone Settings
3. Tap Screen Time
4. Tap Always Allowed
5. Add all essential apps you don't want Scrolless to interrupt. You will still get a notification to take
rest, but those apps won't ever be locked by Scrolless

What if screen locks and break reminders aren't working at all?
Scrolless uses iOS Screen Time to calculate when it's time for you to rest your eyes. Occasionally, iOS
issues may cause Screen Time settings to freeze, which may result in breaks not working for some or all
apps.
This may happen in the following ways:
● You're in a tracked app for a prolonged period, but no screen lock or break reminder appears
● You're not using any tracked app, yet they remain restricted
● You've taken a break as invited, but selected apps are still restricted
Here's how to fix the issue:
Step 1: Reload restrictions
Open any restricted app, then tap on the notification you receive and follow the suggested steps (take a
break or skip it). If this doesn't resolve the issue:
Step 2: Open and close the Scrolless app
Open and then force close the Scrolless app and open it again to reset it. If you're unsure how to do so,
please follow the steps here. If that doesn't fix the issue:
Step 3: Restart your device
Turn your device off and back on. If that doesn't work:
Step 4: Reset Screen Time tracking
If none of the above steps work, reset Scrolless' connection to Screen Time. To do this:
1. Open the Scrolless app
2. Tap on Tracked apps (tap the Profile tab at the top-right corner to access them)
3. Unselect all tracked apps
4. Tap Done
5. Confirm resetting your progress with Yes
6. Then again tap on Tracked apps and select apps you want to track anew
Note: Unfortunately, resetting your Screen Time tracking will reset your Eye Strain Meter to 0 for that
day. This is because this data is private and only available through Screen Time connection. Scrolless
does not store this data anywhere.
Still experiencing issues? Contact our support team - we're here to help!
Account & Billing

How do I cancel my subscription or request a refund?
We're sorry that Scrolless didn't work out for you, and we want to make leaving as smooth as possible!
Before you go, if you're experiencing any bugs or confusing issues, please contact our Support team.
We'll be happy to help.
Important: Uninstalling the Scrolless app will NOT automatically cancel your subscription.
To cancel your subscription:
1. Open your iPhone Settings
2. Tap your Apple ID name
3. Tap Subscriptions
4. Tap Scrolless
5. Tap Cancel Subscription
About timing: Apple may take up to 24 hours to process cancellations. Cancel your free trial or
subscription at least 24 hours before renewal to avoid charges.
About refunds: Scrolless cannot cancel subscriptions or process refunds for purchases made via Apple.
All cancellations and refunds are handled directly by Apple. If you need a refund, request it through
Apple using the instructions here.
Please note that Scrolless Support cannot intervene if Apple rejects your refund request.
Contact & Support

How do I contact Scrolless?
We're here to help!
Get Support:
● Contact Form: Submit a support request
● Email: info@scrolless.com
● Social Media: Message us on any of our channels
When contacting support, please include a detailed description of your issue along with screenshots or
videos when possible. This helps us understand and resolve your problem faster.
Other Inquiries:
For press or media, product feedback and suggestions, partnerships or career opportunities, email us at
info@scrolless.com
Quick Links
● Privacy Policy
● Terms of Service
● App Store Page
● Follow us on Social Media
FAQ’S FOR CLIENT SUPPORT BOT ONLY
I don’t get rest reminders when I’m using the Chrome browser.
We are investigating a recurring issue where screen time spent on a third-party browser (for example,
Chrome) does not trigger breaks reliably. The issue relates to the interoperability of some third-party
browsers with iOS and its Screen Time feature. Until the issue is resolved, you may use Apple's native
browser, Safari, which works reliably with iPhone Screen Time. No issues have been reported from users
using Mozilla Firefox as their browser either.

I'm getting rest reminders too often. It's annoying. Can I change the frequency of reminders?
Most often it's only the feeling that the reminders are too frequent, because you easily lose track of time
when you're on your phone.
Depending on your overall screen time on that day, you get break reminders at intervals of 10 to 20
minutes of uninterrupted screen time.
Presently you cannot manually change the frequency of reminders — it is calculated by our algorithm.
The less you scroll, the less often you will get reminders.

I’m getting rest reminders too seldom. Can I change the frequency off reminders?
Depending on your overall screen time on that day, you get break reminders at intervals of 10 to 20
minutes of uninterrupted screen time. If you mostly use your phone in short intervals (for example, you
check and quickly respond to messages every couple of minutes) and rarely have long scrolling sessions,
you won't get many break reminders.
Presently you cannot manually change the frequency of reminders — it is calculated by our algorithm.
The more natural breaks you take yourself (even if short), the less often Scrolless will ask you to take
one.
Scrolless is most useful for those who strain their eyes during long scrolling sessions. If you want to limit
the number of quick phone pickups, Scrolless might not be the best tool for you.
If I have a long video call on Teams or Google Meet, Scrolless switches me off even if I press skip on a
break. I then need to reconnect to the meeting again.
We understand it's annoying to be disturbed while working on your essential apps. There are two ways
to avoid this: Option 1) do not track time spent in those apps at all; or 2) add those apps to the Always
Allowed list in your phone Settings.
[same as above]
Option 1
Unselect the apps you don't want to be disturbed on in your Tracked Apps list in Scrolless Settings (tap
the Profile tab in the top-right corner).
You won't get Scrolless screen locks or break reminders while using these apps. However, keep in mind
that time spent on these apps will not be included in your overall eye strain calculation, nor reflected in
daily Eye Strain Meter values.
Option 2
Your iPhone's Screen Time Settings have an Always Allowed list of apps, which Apple generates
automatically and to which you can add apps manually. Apps in this category will never be locked by
Scrolless, but time spent on them will still count toward your overall eye strain calculation.
To use this option:
1. Select all apps and categories for Scrolless to track in Scrolless Settings
2. Go to your iPhone Settings
3. Tap Screen Time
4. Tap Always Allowed
5. Add all essential apps you don't want Scrolless to interrupt. You will still get a notification to take
rest, but those apps won't ever be locked by Scrolless

What do I need to do during the scrolling break?
The 20-second break is meant for you to rest your eyes from the screen.
Presently, the Scrolless default rest option suggests simply closing your eyes and relaxing your eye
muscles.
You may swap the break intervals for other rest options. You may look into the distance if you have the
opportunity — 20 feet, or approximately 5 meters away, is the widely recommended distance. If you
have a window, try to look even further, or into nature — that's even better for your eyes.
Alternatively, you may calmly and slowly blink for the 20 seconds, making sure each blink is complete.
You will find many other tips for simple eye rest exercises in the app. Give them a try and see which feels
most comfortable for your eyes. Choose what feels natural and pleasant. Don't overdo it — be gentle
with yourself. The eye is a delicate organ.

During the break Should I watch Scrolless animation for 20 seconds?
No, you don’t have to watch Scrolless animation during your 20 seconds break.
The 20-second break is meant for you to rest your eyes from the screen.
Presently, the Scrolless default rest option suggests simply closing your eyes and relaxing your eye
muscles.
You may swap the break intervals for other rest options. You may look into the distance if you have the
opportunity — 20 feet, or approximately 5 meters away, is the widely recommended distance. If you
have a window, try to look even further, or into nature — that's even better for your eyes.
Alternatively, you may calmly and slowly blink for the 20 seconds, making sure each blink is complete.
You will find many other tips for simple eye rest exercises in the app. Give them a try and see which feels
most comfortable for your eyes. Choose what feels natural and pleasant. Don't overdo it — be gentle
with yourself. The eye is a delicate organ.
At night, can I dim the Scrolless exercise because it’s too bright or it disturbs my partner when I scroll

in bed?
Scrolless uses your preset screen brightness and light or dark mode You can immediately reduce your
iPhone's screen brightness through Control Center at any time. Alternatively, you may adjust your
display’s Appearance, Brightness and set Night Shift through your Phones Settings in Display &
Brightness section.

Does the Eye Strain Meter show accurate strain levels of my eyes?
We do our best to reflect your eye strain as accurately as possible, based on how long and how often
you're using your phone. That said, the meter can only measure what it has access to, so a few things it
can't account for:
● Time spent on other screens, like your laptop, TV, or tablet
● Your existing eye health or diagnosed conditions
● Lighting conditions or screen brightness settings
● Sleep quality, hydration, or general fatigue
● Other factors like age or prescription changes
The Eye Strain Meter gives you a useful picture of how your time on the phone affects your eyes.
Scrolless is not a medical device, and the meter shouldn't replace advice from your eye care professional.

Can Scrolless help end my screen addiction?
Scrolless's primary role is helping you build a healthier relationship with your phone by protecting your
eyes from excessive scrolling. Through the Eye Strain Meter and regular rest breaks, you'll naturally
become more aware of how much time you're spending on screens and how that affects you. Many
users find this awareness alone helps them cut back - no one loves seeing their Eye Strain Meter hitting
extreme red day after day.
That said, Scrolless isn't a traditional screen time control app. We don't set screen time limits or block
apps. Our approach is care, not control: we help you protect your eyes while you use your phone, rather
than trying to restrict how you use it.
